events.label: reasoning
events.text:
><function_calls>
<invoke name="write_file">
<parameter name="path">/app/answer.json</parameter>
<parameter name="content">{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Insufficient torque on electrical terminations/splices causing elevated resistance and thermal rise detectable by IR.",
    "Loose or under-torqued connections create a fire and equipment damage hazard under load.",
    "Condition violates NEC 110.14 requirement for tight connections to ensure low resistance and prevent overheating."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuit/equipment before rework.",
    "Remove, inspect, and re-terminate or re-splice the affected connections using manufacturer-specified torque values.",
    "Use an appropriately calibrated torque wrench or torque screwdriver for all terminations.",
    "Re-inspect with IR thermography after re-energization under load to confirm thermal anomaly is resolved.",
    "Document torque values and re-inspection results on the QC/inspection form."
  ],
  "rationale": "An infrared thermal signature indicating insufficient torque on terminations or splices is a high-risk defect. NEC 110.14 requires electrical connections to be tight and of suitable ampacity; under-torqued connections increase contact resistance, generate heat, and can lead to failure or fire. Because the work is rough-complete, the defect must be corrected and re-verified before close-out.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
